facts.text: The 1970s are often called the peak of the New Hollywood era, when directors gained unusual creative freedom.
The Godfather (1972) won Best Picture and became one of the most acclaimed films ever made.
The Exorcist (1973) became the first horror film ever nominated for Best Picture.
Jaws opened in 1975 and is widely credited with creating the modern summer blockbuster model.
One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest won the 'Big Five' Oscars for Best Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay.
Rocky (1976) turned Sylvester Stallone into a star and won Best Picture.
Taxi Driver (1976) gave the decade one of its most haunting portraits of urban alienation.
Star Wars premiered on May 25, 1977, and transformed visual effects, merchandising, and blockbuster filmmaking forever.
Saturday Night Fever (1977) helped turn disco culture into a global movie and music phenomenon.
Close Encounters of the Third Kind (1977) showed Steven Spielberg could make wonder feel as cinematic as fear.
Superman: The Movie (1978) convinced audiences that comic-book heroes could work in serious big-budget films.
Halloween (1978) helped define the slasher template with a tiny budget and enormous influence.
Alien (1979) fused horror and science fiction into one of the most enduring movie franchises ever.
Apocalypse Now (1979) became legendary both for its war-on-screen imagery and its chaotic production history.
The Deer Hunter and Coming Home showed how deeply the Vietnam War shaped 1970s American cinema.
Grease (1978) became one of the most successful movie musicals of all time.
The Godfather Part II (1974) is one of the rare sequels to win Best Picture.
Directors like Coppola, Scorsese, Spielberg, Lucas, Friedkin, and Altman defined the decade's American filmmaking identity.
1970s movies often featured antiheroes, moral ambiguity, and grittier endings than earlier studio-era films.
The blockbuster success of Jaws and Star Wars also set the stage for Hollywood's franchise-first future.
The Godfather (1972) became one of the most acclaimed crime dramas in movie history.
Its performances, dialogue, and visual style helped make it a permanent fixture on greatest-film lists.
The Godfather is one of the signature titles or subjects people mention when discussing 70s Movies.
